{
  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus primarily on (A) writing polish and clarity (line-level edits, grammar, concision) or (B) research substance (logic, evidence-claim alignment, section structure, and novelty), or (C) both with B prioritized over A?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section claims a contribution and motivation, so reframing or clarifying the thesis and gap may be needed to ensure the core idea and contribution are explicit.",
    "R1.2": "The argument linking queue-aware admission and the two-level scheduler to tail-latency improvements should be checked for internal logical coherence.",
    "R1.3": "Performance and attribution claims reference §§3.2/4, so the review should assess whether the claims are appropriately tied to reported results and described methods.",
    "R1.4": "The \"first\" co-design claim warrants a high-level novelty assessment against the general landscape (without a full literature audit).",
    "R1.5": "The flow from motivation to architecture to summary claims may benefit from section-level reordering or clearer transitions.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity and concision can be improved to reduce run-ons and enhance readability.",
    "RX.1": "The review should note uncertainty due to only seeing this section and not the full evidence in §§3.2/4.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaim risks (e.g., \"first\" and causal attributions) must be flagged and tempered unless well-supported.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback must stay within reviewing this section rather than proposing new experiments or implementation changes.",
    "RX.4": "Suggestions should avoid enabling risky operational practices or misuse in multi-LLM deployment contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should reference specific sentences or cited sections to maintain traceability and provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus primarily on (A) writing polish and clarity (line-level edits, grammar, concision) or (B) research substance (logic, evidence-claim alignment, section structure, and novelty), or (C) both with B prioritized over A?

rationale.R1.1: The section claims a contribution and motivation, so reframing or clarifying the thesis and gap may be needed to ensure the core idea and contribution are explicit.
rationale.R1.2: The argument linking queue-aware admission and the two-level scheduler to tail-latency improvements should be checked for internal logical coherence.
rationale.R1.3: Performance and attribution claims reference §§3.2/4, so the review should assess whether the claims are appropriately tied to reported results and described methods.
rationale.R1.4: The "first" co-design claim warrants a high-level novelty assessment against the general landscape (without a full literature audit).
rationale.R1.5: The flow from motivation to architecture to summary claims may benefit from section-level reordering or clearer transitions.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity and concision can be improved to reduce run-ons and enhance readability.
rationale.RX.1: The review should note uncertainty due to only seeing this section and not the full evidence in §§3.2/4.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim risks (e.g., "first" and causal attributions) must be flagged and tempered unless well-supported.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback must stay within reviewing this section rather than proposing new experiments or implementation changes.
rationale.RX.4: Suggestions should avoid enabling risky operational practices or misuse in multi-LLM deployment contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should reference specific sentences or cited sections to maintain traceability and provenance.
